name: FARO Paper A claim ledger
scope: Submission-facing claims for the FARO method paper.

allowed_claims.claim: FARO reframes source/concept removal as certified edit-or-abstain selection over a leakage-utility frontier.
FARO specifies the candidate family, frontier, certified safe set, lexicographic selection rule, and abstention report.
The theory section proves safe acceptance, simultaneous frontier certification, finite-sample validation certificates, and false-acceptance control for finite audited frontiers.
Waterbirds and Camelyon17-WILDS are the current durable official claim-ready benchmark families in the local packet.
Waterbirds is an official abstention and failure-analysis row where FARO abstains and group-reweighted ERM is stronger.
Camelyon17-WILDS is a claim-ready official high-stakes frozen-embedding benchmark row with an explicit non-clinical-deployment boundary.
The synthetic overlap certificate demonstrates an empty safe set when source removal exceeds the target-preservation budget.

The full Camelyon17-WILDS projection-frontier certificate demonstrates a validation-only ABSTAIN decision because no tested source-direction projection strength certifies the required source reduction.

A prior CivilComments-WILDS stress frontier demonstrates strict abstention and relaxed editing under predeclared budgets, but it is not durable claim-ready evidence until the receipt and statistics are regenerated or rehydrated.

The upstream inventory pins official repositories for MANCE++, R-LACE, TaCo, and LEACE, while current SPLINCE/SPLICE, R-LACE, TaCo, and LEACE rows remain proxy stress tests unless matched receipts are added.
MANCE++ has official-code reference evidence on Waterbirds and a full no-cap claim-grade Camelyon17 reference row under FARO's frozen-representation protocol.

The local packet is internally main-track ready with green readiness, reproducibility, claim-ledger, and adversarial-review audits, while still denying universal erasure state-of-the-art and clinical deployment claims.
Paper A has a checked reproducibility packet covering seed policy, commands, artifact map, release boundaries, and two durable official claim-ready rows.

forbidden_claims.claim: FARO beats group-reweighted ERM on Waterbirds.
forbidden_exact_patterns: FARO beats group-reweighted ERM on Waterbirds
FARO outperforms group-reweighted ERM on Waterbirds

forbidden_claims.claim: FARO is clinically safe or ready for deployment.
forbidden_exact_patterns: FARO is ready for clinical deployment

forbidden_claims.claim: FARO is state of the art across all domain-generalization or concept-erasure benchmarks.
forbidden_exact_patterns: FARO is state of the art across all domain-generalization benchmarks
FARO is state of the art across all concept-erasure benchmarks

forbidden_claims.claim: FARO works without source or environment labels.
forbidden_exact_patterns: FARO works without source or environment labels
FARO does not require source labels

forbidden_claims.claim: Camelyon17 evidence establishes clinical safety or deployment readiness.
forbidden_exact_patterns: Camelyon17 is ready for clinical deployment

forbidden_claims.claim: The current proxy SPLINCE/SPLICE, R-LACE, TaCo, and MANCE rows are official reference implementations.

external_blockers: CivilComments-WILDS receipt/statistical-report regeneration or rehydration remains required before it can count as a durable local official row.
Local AAAI PDF compilation still requires PDFLaTeX before an actual AAAI upload.
Exact matched reference receipts for R-LACE, TaCo, LEACE, and SPLINCE/SPLICE remain optional strengthening beyond the scoped protocol claim.
